entry.title: Re: Customer master data migration - Dunning level and Last Dunned date field values are not require

entry.title: SAP Subscription Billing now available on the Customer Influence Portal
entry.content:
In the past, requirements related to SAP Subscription Billing had to be posted through an SAP S/4HANA Cloud component.
Now, there is a new category for Subscription Billing to collect your requirements and feedback. 

Just follow this link to post your request.
If you want to learn more about the Customer Influence and Adoption programs, follow this link.

entry.title: Re: Cancel goods issue as material has not reached the cu...
entry.content:
Hello @AAkash1 

Technically, you can cancel both a billing document with VF11 and a goods issue with VL09.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Master data load into S4Hana or MDG?
entry.content:
@venkateshgollaThanks for this valuable input.

Loading into MDG can happen via Data Migration Cockpit or specific loading tiles.

The Data Migration Cockpit templates structure (tables and fields) are available in the tables of "/1LT/" domain.

Do you know if such description is available at database level for MDG loading tiles ?

Best regards

Do any one have implemented create header to item relation with unmanged scenario where 

The "create" operation cannot be enabled for an association ("project") that is "to parent" or for which "dependent by" is used.

entry.title: Re: Ariba PO Number Range Configuration
entry.content:
Hello, 

Approvabletypes.csv import allows to change it but is not available to end-users.

You need a ticket with support.

Take a look at resolution of this support note to get an idea:

https://support.ariba.com/item/view/KB0395300

entry.title: Re: System News: Customizing the My Home news feed
entry.content: @Costiantyn_M works like a charme! Thank you

entry.title: SAP CAR UDF: Running Modelling and Forecasting at Virtual (dummy) store level
entry.content:
Hi All,

We're currently implementing SAP UDF for a retail customer in the online channel. The customer has a centralized supply chain setup, where a single distribution center in one country supplies to customers in multiple countries, each assigned to a different distribution chain.

For example, our distribution center in Sweden supplies to customers in Norway, Denmark, and Iceland, which are assigned to distribution chains 100-10, 200-10, and 300-10, respectively.

We've set up virtual (dummy) stores for each distribution chain and are replicating sale orders from ECC to CAR using the DRF framework (PWEB object). These sales orders are replicated to the CAR /DMF/PS_TS table with virtual store mapping based on the distribution chain.

However, we're facing a challenge when running modeling and forecasting. We're unable to run these processes at the virtual store level, which is necessary since each distribution chain (country) has unique holidays and requirements.

We need to find a way to run modeling and forecasting for each virtual store to accurately capture the demand patterns for each distribution chain.

I'd love to hear your thoughts and any other ideas you may have. 

Regards,

Masilamani

entry.title: Re: Training, practice, free usage of system
entry.content: You may not access the learning tutorials. But for example if you want to learn SAP BTP. SAP provide demo environment for practice. Whereas SAP Hybris if you want to try there is no such option to learn except going thru with the documentation

entry.title: Re: Clear Open PMR from "Stage for Production" app
entry.content:
Well, the TECO mechanism the SAP standard for this purpose. 

If you want to filter the relevant PMR items by other attributes you should think about implementing the BAdI /SCWM/EX_MFG_STAGE_INFO. The fallback class of this BAdI is the SAP standard algorithm for the staging proposals. The fallback class is not final, so you can easily adjust parts of it.

entry.title: Re: Company Code without Controlling Area Assignment
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you're correct: there is only one controlling area (A000), and all company codes are assigned to this single controlling area by design. This is part of the standard configuration of the Cloud Public Edition to ensure simplification and standardization across financial and controlling processes.

 

So, to answer your question directly:

No, it is not possible to create a company code that is not assigned to any controlling area in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Why?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition uses a universal journal and integrated financial architecture, which mandates that all company codes must be part of the global controlling structure to enable end-to-end processes (like reporting, allocations, and planning) across Finance and Controlling.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Company Code without Controlling Area Assignment
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

 

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you're correct: there is only one controlling area (A000), and all company codes are assigned to this single controlling area by design. This is part of the standard configuration of the Cloud Public Edition to ensure simplification and standardization across financial and controlling processes.

 

So, to answer your question directly:

No, it is not possible to create a company code that is not assigned to any controlling area in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Why?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition uses a universal journal and integrated financial architecture, which mandates that all company codes must be part of the global controlling structure to enable end-to-end processes (like reporting, allocations, and planning) across Finance and Controlling.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Assistance with Handling Overlapping Sales Order and Customer Number Entities in SAP CAI
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently facing a challenge in handling two entities, Sales Orders and Customer Numbers, within SAP Conversational AI (CAI). Both entities follow a similar pattern, consisting of digits in a 10-digit format.

To define these entities, I have set up the Sales Order as a regex entity with the pattern \d{10}, and the Customer Number as a restricted entity, with a list of values fetched from SAP. However, I am encountering difficulties in resolving the overlapping patterns between these two entities, as both consist of numeric digits and can vary in length from 1 to 10 digits.

Could you kindly provide guidance on the following:

How should I define the Sales Order entity to correctly handle varying input lengths, from 1 to 10 digits?

What approach should I take to ensure the bot processes these entities accurately and allows for the required input to be captured one by one?

I would appreciate any insights or suggestions on how to structure these entities and resolve the current issue.

Thank you for your time, and I look forward to your response.

Thanks!!

entry.title: Re: Error Handling - SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit
entry.content:
Hello, thank you for your message and for sharing the screenshot!

Just to clarify: the screenshot you shared is likely coming from the “Monitoring” or “Activities with Error” view, which is not sorted by default. When exported to Excel, the same unsorted structure is retained.

If you're looking for a more structured or sorted view, we recommend using the “Messages” button. The Migration Cockpit offers multiple options for reviewing errors — depending on whether you're analyzing by instance, message type, or other dimensions. These views are explained in detail in the Cookbook.

We’re continuously working on improvements, and some updates are already planned for future releases.

If you have specific suggestions for how this process should work differently, we encourage you to submit them via the SAP Customer Influence Program — this helps us track and prioritize ideas based on wider customer interest. (S-user login required; if needed, see KBA 2676589.)

Best regards,

Elizaveta

entry.title: Re: External Contingent Workforce for Customer Projects
entry.content:
Great blog!
I would like to understand when at the end of the month the invoice for the total hours worked in a month comes from the subcontractor, how can this invoice be posted against the goods receipt if there is a separate goods receipt for each individual time recording of the month?

entry.title: Re: CDP Data Access>>Exports feature
entry.content:
Hi Owen,

As of today, the customer data platform console’s export functionality (under Data Access > Exports) only supports full exports, and delta exports with scheduling option is not available.
Since delta export is a valuable use case, I recommend submitting a feature request outlining your business needs under this link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3427
SAP Product Management team will review the request and respond accordingly.
Hope this is helpful.thanks

Regards,
Anand Raj

entry.title: Sensitive compensation data RBP subjected not hidden during the hiring approval wf
entry.content:
Hi,

we are encountering an issue when enhancing our EC configuration with some custom firlds in job info and in comp info portlet dedicated to very sensitive topics . We hardly worked to discriminate visibility and editability over those fields via RBP.

But the same fields are visible when hiring a new person in the workflow approval . The effect is that hiring approvers, set by role to do not have those fields in visibility, isntead can see fiends and content in the hiring approval workflow.

We openea ah hign priority tk to SAP, but it looks like this is the expected behaviour. Se also note https://me.sap.com/notes/3577609 .

We are looking for a workaround solution or for a suggestion to bypass it. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Unable to read repository at https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest/content.xml.xz.
entry.content: Not entirely sure but could be that your traffic is routed through a proxy or VPN and therefor complaining about the certification path. You would have to manually enter the proxy and/or install the certificate.

entry.title: Re: How to Create Custom Tile for Custom Analytical Queries and Extend it using Custom Catalog Exten
entry.content:
@bradly1618   Thanks for content

Can we create a Custom Business Catalog  in sap public cloud  and make use of Custom Business catalog for assigning it instead of standard catalog under Add section ?

entry.title: Purchase order with history release strategy is restarted
entry.content:
Hi Experts

The release strategy of the released purchase order with partial goods receipt is restarted when the net amount of the purchase order is reduced.

The reduction of the amount happens because some of the items were cancelled or the quantity purchased in the item with partial attention was reduced and the new amount is in the range of another release strategy.

In the configuration of the release strategy for the release indicator is assigned the value 4 in the modification option

The business requirement is that the release strategy is only restarted when the purchase order has no goods receipt or service entry sheet. Even if the purchase order is not yet fulfilled, the release strategy should not be restarted if the new reduced amount is in the range of another strategy.

I look forward to your support if the above can be achieved with the standard configuration or if customization is required.

Best regards Percy

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Hi

Thank you for great blog.

I have a question about CloudIntegration destination. 
Where this destination is used or can be configured? 

What in case if we have two separate CPI landscapes? 
Should we create dedicated TMS for each landscape ?  

Reg.
Artur

entry.title: Re: Promotion in Analyze Promotions App is not editable
entry.content:
I have raised ticket to SAP.  The issue in my promotion was, that only one product from the all products, mentioned in promotion has values, all others not. According to the IBP functionality it is not possible to create new values in already created promotion, this is why it is locked for editing.

Reason why only one product has values is, that promotion has been created not in base unit of measure and only one this product has conversion maintained.

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Analytic stories - Drill option in table and charts
entry.content: Hi, this topics seems unanswered and I actually have the same issue. Has anyone found out yet?

entry.title: Re: Pre-Carriage and On-Carriage on Freight Booking
entry.content: Hi Ajit, thanks for the prompt response (as always :)). My problem is because we need to prebook capacity with the carrier months in advance and so we need to create the freight booking first. Unfortunately, the freight booking just won't expose the pre-carriage and on-carriage leg for manual entry. Interesting, if I create a container TU and create the FB from the TU, the pre-carriage and on-carriage stage are included. Regards SFC

entry.content:
Hi Experts

In S/4HANA, as standard, it is possible to convert stock transport requisitions to stock transport orders using transaction ME59N, just as is done with purchase requisitions for goods.

If possible you can help me by telling me in a general way the main configurations to be done and if you could share me a user manual it would be great.

Best regards Percy

entry.title: Re: Drill option of table disabled in stories
entry.content: Hello, how do I open the model? The question is for IBP Analytics Stories only. The drill down button remains grey. Thank you

entry.title: Re: CPI to CPI Connection and both tenants will be on different subaccounts at different regions.
entry.content:
Hi @Yogita1 ,

Communication between both CPI's is similar to communication between an external web application and CPI, so you don't have limitations about the number of calls. It is important to take into account the licensing model for messages, because a huge volume scenario can has an important impact: https://me.sap.com/notes/2942344

About latency, you must consider the location (cloud provider and region) where each CPI is deployed.

About payload size, here you have some information:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/optimize-performance

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/limit-size-of-incoming-messages 

Hope it helps!

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: Unable to read repository at https://tools.hana.ondem...
entry.content:
yes like already mentioned below , in your Eclipse, please check proxy settings.

Window → Preferences → General → Network Connections

if on proxy, please give proxy details.

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Queries on CLT: Myanmar - GL settings
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding CLT and rollout to Myanmar - GL settings:

1. As Germany will be the source country for Myanmar, and DEAP is the local ledger for Germany, is it necessary to assign DEAP against the Myanmar company code ? , or will the local ledger for Myanmar be available?

2. When should Germany be activated? Should Germany be activated from the start even if the first rollout outs are for standard Asian localized countries? Or should Germany be activated only when the rollout to Myanmar happens? 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Duarte

entry.title: Not able to create tarball file in Java based CAPM project
entry.content:
I am following one tutorial for Java based CAPM project.

Below command is not working

npm pack creates a tarball from the products-service, which is then directly used as a dependency in the bookstore application. Learn more about npm pack.

You will find a sap-capire-products-1.0.0.tgz in the root folder of the bookstore project, which is the tarball file of the products-service project.

Everyone with a subscription to SAP Learning Hub is free to register.

entry.title: Re: Quantities sold per warehouse
entry.content:
Hi Jeroen,

Please give this a try:

 

SELECT
T0.[CreateDate],
T0.[ItemCode],
T0.[ItemName],
/* Replace the 1, 2, and 3 in r.WhsCode='1' with the actual warehouse codes: */
isnull((select sum(r.Quantity) from INV1 r inner join OINV h on r.DocEntry = h.DocEntry where r.ItemCode = T0.[ItemCode] and r.WhsCode='1'), 0) AS WAREHOUSE1,
isnull((select sum(r.Quantity) from INV1 r inner join OINV h on r.DocEntry = h.DocEntry where r.ItemCode = T0.[ItemCode] and r.WhsCode='2'), 0) AS WAREHOUSE2,
isnull((select sum(r.Quantity) from INV1 r inner join OINV h on r.DocEntry = h.DocEntry where r.ItemCode = T0.[ItemCode] and r.WhsCode='3'), 0) AS WAREHOUSE3

FROM 
OITM T0 

WHERE 
T0.[CreateDate] <= dateadd(year , -1, getdate()))

ORDER BY
1 DESC

Regards,

Johan

},

entry.title: Re: Custom UIBB Not Visible in sap mdg fpm
entry.content: Hi Jen, Thanks for reply. I tried in mentioned IF_FPM_OVP->CHANGE_UIBB , where in field hidden is not 'X' during the debug flow. Then i deleted the personalization as well. Can i know is there any additional setting i need to check in the system. Thanks for supporting on this.

entry.title: Do you want to explore more about the extensibility mechanisms in SAP Digital Manufacturing?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Customization in SAP Digital Manufacturing learning journey.

Overview
This course aims consultants, solution architects and industry specialists, who want to explore more about the extensibility mechanisms in SAP Digital Manufacturing. The course will especially focus on the Production Operator Dashboard Designer and the Production Process Designer.

Learning objectives

  • Design Production Operator Dashboards (PODs)
  • Understand POD Designer and components
  • Learn about Production Process Designer
  • Create and manage production processes
  • Handle runtime errors and apply log levels
  • Monitor and debug production processes
  • Leverage the Service Registry
  • Publish processes and register APIs
  • Identify triggers and create business rules
  • Create custom POD buttons and notifications

Prerequisites

    • Discovering SAP Digital Manufacturing
    • Configuring SAP Digital Manufacturing for Execution: Basic Data and Configuration
    • Interest in basic programming topics

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to use bseg and for all entries in
entry.content: So, you'll have to use two SELECT: one for BKPF and BSIS, one for BSEG.

entry.title: Re: CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET creating two addition sheets by default
entry.content: You may use either abap2xlsx or XCO instead of CL_FDT_XL_SPREADSHEET.

entry.title: Re: CAP Fiori Elements Invoking Custom Action (err: Unkno...
entry.content: I found the way, instead of "function" I changed to "action" in CDS, now everything is fine!

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA MM - Mapping between purchasing group to mat...
entry.content: Correct path is :SPRO --> MM --> Purchasing --> Purchase Requisition --> Define Mapping of Material Group to Purchasing group

entry.title: BTP Integration Suite_Service Contract screen IF error message
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

Service Contract information is linked to other peripheral systems using BTP Integration Suite.
When a Service Contract is created, BTP will be launched and data will be converted and integrated.

【Requirements】
When you press the "Save" button on the S/4HANA Cloud Service Contract, it is expected that IF integration will begin.
If an error occurs during IF integration, is it possible to display something like an error message on the Service Contract screen?

Soma

entry.title: Re: Label in F4 is not getting displayed for the fiori lo...
entry.content: Issue got resolved after updating the label name in odata service.

entry.title: Re: Pre-Carriage and On-Carriage on Freight Booking
entry.content:
Hi @SFC,

Thank you for sharing your business scenario in community,.

Great! This time you answered your own question:)

Kind Regards

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Hello Artur,

The destination is required to point to your source SAP Cloud Integration instance, on your source subaccount, to define the source endpoint. You can also check out TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Con... - SAP Community (and especially the comments from Gauravi):

For your second question: you can use one cTMS instance to handle different environments - and you would then create a dedicated transport landscape for each CPI landscape (then pointing via the different destinations to the different end points running on different CPI tenants).

I hope this helps!

Best regards,
Boris

entry.title: Re: Text in Salesorder, Which Table does it get saved
entry.content: same issue, any solution?

entry.title: Re: Question: Is ABAP performance tracing (ST12) still a...
entry.content:
I rarely use ST12 because I find the User Interface difficult to understand. I prefer using ST05 and SAT directly, depending on the context. If I remember well, some options of ST05 or SAT were not available in ST12. I use ST12 only if I have to start ST05 and SAT at the same time, but it happens rarely.

Many of my ABAP colleagues prefer using ST12, so I have asked them to look at your question.

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Hello Boris 

We have config 


And with this configuration, transport created and released from DEV NEW, in cTMS is visible as a created on DEV OLD.
I guess that responsible for this is the connection - CloudIntegration - correct me if I'm wrong. (docs https://help.sap.com/docs/content-agent-service/user-guide/create-sap-cloud-integration-destination?q=CloudIntegration)
Can we create CloudIntegration_OLD and CloudIntegration_NEW connections and force use depending on which CPI transport is created/released  by some additional parameters ?

Regards

Artur

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Message Mapping - addContextChange in UDF is ignored
entry.content: Why write code when not necessary at all? The index approach will still handle the case when the source node does not exist by not producing target output, and it doesn't require maintaining any code directly. The goal should not be to write code but rather solve a problem, which in this case can be done with zero lines of code.

entry.title: Re: crystal report issue
entry.content:
When you use Windows Authentication you need to design and enable Trusted Authentication in the Report when connecting to the DB.

If it's not done this way then only option is to log in using SQL Server Authentication.

entry.title: Different number ranges for sales document type and billing document type.
entry.content:
Hello Team,

In public cloud, We create sales order with (OR) Order type, as there is a requirement from customer for multiple sales order type copying OR order type which is not possible in public cloud system. so we have created one custom field Order sub type in that we have maintained multiple sales document type, so the user can select the Sub Order Type as required and can create a sales order, now the requirement is we want different number ranges for Order sub type and same should work for billing document.

Please suggest how can we achieve this scenario.

entry.title: Re: Downloads link in course accelerate-enterprise-integr...
entry.content: Thanks @katrin_elk for the workaround! It works.

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content: Hi @Alex_D same here, at DSAG Tech days i got told that we should open up an incident. So we did and the response was that "there seems to be a delay with the provisioning of the item". But no more than that. Regards

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: Ah yes, right, only required when a LB is used. Likely cause then is threading issue, you need to keep track of which thread/IIS Instance the report is using and make sure any requests go back to that IIS Process.

entry.title: Re: Show backend errors in SAPUI5 app using bapiret2_t
entry.content: How to declare mo_context ?

entry.content:
Hi, we have the same challenge and we have not come up with a solution but I saw this blog with a concept that we are looking at to meet this requirement. Maybe it can work for this scenario?

Termination processes and when Offboarding can be ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Retroacitve Billing in S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: As per this SAP Help documentation, it is possible.  Of course, it is for price difference.

entry.title: Re: Create 2 bits from a cit but to different business pa...
entry.content:
Hello

First in order to create severals BITs, I suggest you to use allowances . By doing that, your main BIT is created by the charge on the main business partner and the allowance will create an additional BIT. 

Then in the allowance plan, you define the Output Item in order to redefine the value GPART and VKONT in order to reach another business partner than the initial one. You can use value from allowance parameters in order to redefine these field values. 

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
We have CAS and cTMS in one subbacount. Maybe the solution is to create two CAS, one per landscape?
Means crate CAS instance on each CPI DEV subbacount.

entry.title: Datasphere - Graphical vs SQL Views. Procs and Cons of each?
entry.content:
This is currently a debate between two developers, one a seasoned SQL developer and the other one who prefers graphical interfaces.

We both have similar arguments - transparency and ease of interpretation; speed; robustness

However you could argue for and against either based on all of the above.

Scouring the web on the topic (but not just related to Datasphere) some of the responses are:

Three reasons you would prefer the GUI:

  • The organisation that owns the dataset wants to protect the data infrastructure from poorly written SQL
  • Someone only asks rudimentary questions on small to medium sized data sets
  • Someone does not know how to write SQL and does not want to learn

Three reasons you would prefer using SQL:

  • SQL allows for more complex queries not easily achievable through the GUI, often with functions not available in the GUI Expression editor
  • Experienced developers can write SQL queries more quickly than navigating the GUI
  • Once written, a SQL query can be amended for another purpose, without starting from scratch

There are some interesting articles out there:

https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1ELHonECz4Z0bgBle7Ld_v2gM8xZFPFKUmjYznKGkCFY/present?slide=id.p1

https://www.nextlytics.com/blog/sap-datasphere-sql-or-graphical-views-making-an-educated-decision

Graphical views may display the relationships between tables in terms of linkages, but you have to click on the joins to see the fields used. You also have to click on projections to see fields excluded, and functions to see transformations. A view with multiple tables, whilst on the surface presents a simplistic view, requires time to fully understand the ‘what’ and ‘how’ of what it is doing.

SQL views, when written applying good coding practices, present everything in one place – the sources, the joins (both the connections and the fields used), the conditions, any transformational logic and which fields are output and with what names/aliases. Including comments can also explained the 'Why' too

The argument that SQL queries can be overly complicated and difficult to interpret can be mitigated by splitting the code into sections, or even separate views. There is no necessity for monster scripts to be produced. Simpler layered views can be used for multiple purposes, GUI or SQL.

To focus purely on one as a corporate approach appears to be throwing the baby out with the bath water, when it should be horses for courses.

On a personal note I am that seasoned SQL developer, which may explain any unintentional bias herein. That said, I have a list of additional pros for SQL, one being:

  • One that recently came to light was rebuilding downstream views because a change was required to the initial view on the source table.
  • For SQL I copied the script, dropped the view, edited the high-level view, re-created the low-level view, pasted the script and made the necessary amendments. 2 minutes
  • For a graphical view, containing 5 tables, this takes much longer and the risk of missing something – a filter in a projection, a transformation in a function – is considerably higher.

I’d be interested to know your thoughts on the matter?

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Message Mapping - addContextChange in UDF is ignored
entry.content: Hi Ryan, I do agree that this would work in my case. But in general I would still like to understand what I did wrong in my coding approach, to understand how it could be used in other cases in the future.

entry.title: Re: Quantities sold per warehouse
entry.content: Thanks mate 🙂

entry.title: Navigate in RAP via Button from root to subnode, not via standard arrow
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a RAP application with a root node and a subnode.  I would like to navigate from the list with all the records of the root nodes, via a button to the detail of the selected root node and the records of the corresponding subnode (so not via the standard arrow).
Is this possible in a simple way?
Is this possible with an ObjectPage?
Is there anyone who can help me with this?

Thx, Helga

entry.title: Re: SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Subscription with BYOD plan
entry.content:
Hello ShubhranshuP,

Please see answers for your questions below:

1. What are the additional Benefits of BYOD over the current 'PaPM Run-Time DB' ?

BYOD and PaPM Run-time DB still works in one code-line. Meaning, absolutely no difference when we are talking about its functionalities(Join, Calculation and so on...). But if the customer is using SAP HANA Cloud or SAP Datasphere as their strategic data platform then BYOD approach will provide additional benefits in terms of seamless integration as the data is already sitting with SAP PaPM Cloud.

The only thing need to perform is to provide the proper grants / authorization to use the data for complex calculation / data process in SAP PaPM Cloud.  For more information see: Administration Guide for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud.

Under Additional Configuration > Database Administration, you will be seeing the scenarios that might be useful for grants.

2. Under a same tenant, can i have both 'PaPM Runtime DB' and 'BYOD' subscriptions?

No, this is not possible.

3. Is there is any migration path available, to move existing developments from  'PaPM Run-Time DB' to BYOD ?

As of this time of my comment, there is no lean migration paths yet from HANA Cloud​
Runtime Database (Default Plan) to Standalone Database (BYOD)​

entry.title: Re: Cancel goods issue as material has not reached the cu...
entry.content:
For cancelling Good issue and Invoice in SAP, you can perform a basic internet search and you will receive necessary details. 

In your case it seems the Good are lost in transit to the Customer. If you reverse the Goods issue they will be posted back to your PLant's stock and they have to be manually written off. 

Rather I would suggest to issue a Credit Note to Customer (if the Goods are list in transit and INvoice was already created). 

Thanks,

entry.title: FG Connector Library Documentation incorrect
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I was trying to report a documentation error but couldn't find a way, hence posting it here.

The documentation at the fieldglass connector library, specifically for the Cost Center Upload connector (Cost Center Upload | SAP Help Portal ) seems to be incorrect.

Under the section File Formatting -> Detail, the column names mention "Cost Center Code", "Cost Center Description" and so on but the connector is expecting "Cost Centre Code", "Cost Centre Description" and so on. Basically, the Center should be changed to Centre. Thank you

Regards,

mak42

entry.title: Re: Custom UIBB Not Visible in sap mdg fpm
entry.content: HI Kartik, I don't know of anything else. If UIBBs are not visible in the OVP which should be visible, then the root cause are either runtime changes that are done by the application or explicit or implicit personalization.

entry.title: Re: Sap Enable Now Launch Error - ZIP Error & FDIError
entry.content:
Thanks for the people who tried to assist me.  After trying every solution that has been previously been given and nothing worked, i deleted my wpb file, copied a colleague's wpb file uploaded it to my temp file and now it works.  Hope this assists some else who in the future also battles endlessly.

Regards

entry.title: Re: My Inbox Showing Blank
entry.content: Hi JCD, probably if you can share how you configured in your system please? I am struggling atm to find ways to configure this...

entry.title: Re: Different number ranges for sales document type and b...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Assigning different number range to sales documents based on custom logic not supported in S/4HANA Cloud. There is no corresponding BAdI to support this extension.

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Enhancement using Clean core approach usi...
entry.content: @Ila99, Check our this BAdI: SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_BUSINESS which will suffice your requirement.

entry.title: Re: How can I deploy my completed SAP Build Apps web appl...
entry.content: Hi Arun,

entry.title: Exploring Custom Business Objects in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 3SL
entry.content:
Hello everyone! This is my first blog post, and I'm excited to share my journey exploring SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 3SL. Recently, I had the opportunity to work with Custom Business Objects (CBOs) in the Customizing Tenant. In this post, I'll walk you through the process of creating entries using the ODATA API of a Custom Business Object within the system.

Creating Custom Business Objects

In our project, we started by creating Custom Business Objects in the Customizing Tenant. This process automatically generated a user interface (UI) that allowed us to create entries directly. Here's a quick overview of the steps:

  1. Navigate to the Customizing Tenant: Access the SAP Fiori launchpad and go to the Custom Business Objects app.
  2. Create a New Custom Business Object: Define the object with the necessary fields and attributes.
  3. Generate the UI: Once the object is created, the system generates a UI for data entry. (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-extensibility-cbo-create..html)

Using the ODATA API

Our next task was to create entries using the ODATA API of the Custom Business Object. This required a few prerequisites:

  1. Create a Custom Communication Service: Ensure you have set up a communication service for your Custom Business Object. (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-business-object..html)
  2. Access the Development Tenant: Log in to the Development Tenant and open Eclipse ADT.

Creating a Class in Eclipse ADT

To interact with the ODATA API, we created a class in Eclipse ADT. Here are the steps:

  1. Open Eclipse ADT: Launch Eclipse and log in to your Development Tenant.
  2. Create a New ABAP Class: Use the following template in your class:

We have created a structure with Code & Reason. To know the HTTP Response for our action.(EX : 201 Created, 400 BADRequest..)

We are getting to the implementation part.

LV_URL will have the ODATA Url which you would have got from the Communication Service of your CBO 

From lv_user_id to lv_last_name are the declaration of values which will be used in the payload.

We use cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url to create an HTTP destination, which represents the connection to the OData service.

  • HTTP Client: An HTTP client is created using cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination.
  • Authorization: Basic authorization is set using set_authorization_basic.
  • Headers: Headers for content type, accept, and CSRF token are set.
  • CSRF Token: The CSRF token is fetched and set in the request header.

The Final Part,

  • Payload: A payload is created with user details and set in the request.
  • POST Request: A POST request is executed to create entries in the Custom Business Object.
  • Response Handling: The response status and reason are captured.

I am creating 3 entries by iteration, so i have used DO statement in it.

To check the result, you can check the CBO Application.

Conclusion

Working with Custom Business Objects in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 3SL has been an enriching experience. The ability to create and manage custom objects, coupled with the power of ODATA APIs, opens up numerous possibilities for customization and integration.

I hope this post helps you get started with Custom Business Objects and ODATA APIs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 3SL. Stay tuned for more insights and tutorials. Cloud Operations SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ABAP Extensibility SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE 16 certification on Oracle Cloud
entry.content:
SAPNote 2922454 is maintained as an entry point for hyperscaler deployments of ASE.  It has links to SAPNotes detailing O/S requirements for ASE 16.0 SP03, SP04 and ASE 16.1 SP00.

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP b1 analytics powered by SAP HANA is not installed...
entry.content:
Steps to Execute:

  1. Run the following command on the full instance:
    ALTER DATABASE NDB ADD 'scriptserver'

  2. After executing the command, open the Control Center (SLD), go to Services, and click on the link Enablement.

  3. Click on the Company icon. You will see your database listed there.
    Select your database and click the Initialize icon.
    Wait until the initialization process is complete.

  4. Once completed, re-login to SAP and check your dashboard — it should now be accessible.

Note:
If the dashboard does not open, please restart the following services:

  • Service Layer

  • Web Client

  • SAP Business One Server Tools Service 

Then, reopen SAP and check again.

Thanks

Himanshu Yadav

entry.title: Re: How to automate a warehouse task creation
entry.content: Hi Ajit, it is a bin to bin for HU

entry.title: Enable Background Elements in SAP Onboarding 2.0 (Enhancement ID: 344613)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I’d like to bring attention to a significant enhancement request that impacts many SAP SuccessFactors customers across industries and geographies.

Current Limitation:

As of today, Background Elements such as:

  • Education

  • Work Experience

  • Certifications

  • Languages, etc.

are not supported in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0. This limitation means such critical data cannot be captured during the onboarding process and does not flow automatically into Employee Central post-hire.

Why This Matters:

This gap creates operational inefficiencies and potential compliance risks. Without Background Elements support in Onboarding:

  • HR teams must manually collect and re-enter data into EC

  • Employees often have to duplicate data entry post-hire

  • Incomplete profiles hinder downstream processes like Learning, Talent, and Analytics

  • Organizations face challenges in maintaining data integrity and audit readiness

Proposed Enhancement (ID: 344613):

The enhancement proposes that SAP:

  1. Enable support for Background Elements in the Onboarding 2.0 process

  2. Allow seamless integration of that data into Employee Central upon hire

This would ensure a complete and accurate employee profile from Day 1, streamline data flow, and reduce manual intervention.

Who Benefits:

  • HR Professionals: Reduce manual workload, ensure data consistency

  • New Hires: Smoother onboarding experience

  • Compliance Teams: Audit-ready certification and education records

  • Managers: Better decision-making with enriched employee data

  • All SAP Customers: More scalable and standardized onboarding-to-hire process

How You Can Help:

Please take a moment to support this enhancement request on the SAP Customer Influence Portal:

Vote here → https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/344613/?section=sectionDetails

Your vote and comments can make a difference in helping SAP prioritize this enhancement that can benefit the entire SuccessFactors ecosystem.

Let’s collaborate and help shape the future of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.

entry.title: Re: You are not authorized to query the remote system. Please ask to grant you the InA role.
entry.content: I Have the same issue , have you addressed the final solution?
